edges.description: Mitochondrial phospholipid disruption contributes to respiratory-chain complex dysfunction.
Cardiac energy failure from impaired oxidative phosphorylation contributes to hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.
Severe cardiomyopathy and bioenergetic failure drive heart failure in Sengers syndrome.
Skeletal muscle oxidative phosphorylation deficiency contributes to mitochondrial myopathy.
Bioenergetic failure in skeletal muscle contributes to poor exercise tolerance.
Respiratory-chain impairment shifts metabolism toward lactate accumulation.
Impaired oxidative phosphorylation is reflected by elevated lactate.

nodes.description: ACE inhibitor therapy used for heart failure management.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: Two Chinese Infants of Sengers Syndrome Caused by Mutations in AGK Gene.
evidence.snippet: Supplementation with coenzyme Q10, carnitine, B-vitamins, and biotin (called mitochondrial cocktail) was given daily, associated with angiotensin converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitors for cardiomyopathy management.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: Two Chinese Infants of Sengers Syndrome Caused by Mutations in AGK Gene.
evidence.snippet: The patient received milrinone, diuretics (furosemide and spironolactone), and captopril to improve heart function.
Sengers syndrome (OMIM #212350) is a rare autosomal recessive disorder due to mutations in acylglycerol kinase (AGK) gene.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: Two Chinese Infants of Sengers Syndrome Caused by Mutations in AGK Gene.
evidence.snippet: After genetic analysis, a novel homozygous (c.1131+2T>C) variant of AGK gene was identified in the proband.

nodes.description: Loss of functional AGK impairs lipid signaling activity attributed to mitochondrial acylglycerol kinase.
nodes.id: AGK loss of function disrupts lipid signaling

evidence.snippet: AGK, a mitochondrial acylglycerol kinase, is not only involved in lipid signaling but is also a component of the TIM22 complex in the inner mitochondrial membrane, which mediates the import of a subset of membrane proteins.

nodes.description: Vitamin B complex supplementation as part of supportive therapy.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: Two Chinese Infants of Sengers Syndrome Caused by Mutations in AGK Gene.
evidence.snippet: Supplementation with coenzyme Q10, carnitine, B-vitamins, and biotin (called mitochondrial cocktail) was given daily, associated with angiotensin converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitors for cardiomyopathy management.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: Two Chinese Infants of Sengers Syndrome Caused by Mutations in AGK Gene.
evidence.snippet: Levocarnitine (100 mg/kg daily), coenzyme Q10 (1 mg/kg daily), and vitamin B complex (vitamin B1 20 mg/day and riboflavin 10 mg/day) were administered to improve metabolic status;

nodes.description: Heart transplantation for severe cardiomyopathy in Sengers syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Cardiac transplantation for hypertrophic cardiomyopathy associated with Sengers syndrome.
evidence.snippet: This report describes cardiac transplantation for the treatment of the cardiomyopathy associated with Sengers' syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: A neonate with a spongy failing heart - What could it be?
evidence.snippet: At 8 months of age, cardiac transplantation was successfully done and baby has been doing well post-transplantation.

evidence.snippet: N-terminal pro-brain natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP) reached 6,076 pg/ml (reference ranges <125 pg/ml) reflecting the state of the heart failure.

nodes.description: AGK loss alters phospholipid metabolism in mitochondrial membranes, contributing to disease pathogenesis.

evidence.snippet: AGK mutations can alter both phospholipid metabolism and mitochondrial protein biogenesis, contributing to the pathogenesis of Sengers syndrome.

nodes.description: Diuretics used for heart failure management in Sengers syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: A neonate with a spongy failing heart - What could it be?
evidence.snippet: Baby was on regular follow-up and was thriving well on diuretics, sacubitril-valsartan and weekly levosimendan infusions.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: Two Chinese Infants of Sengers Syndrome Caused by Mutations in AGK Gene.
evidence.snippet: The clinical presentation includes congenital cataracts, hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, skeletal myopathy, exercise intolerance, and lactic acidosis.
Both infants had typical clinical features characterized by hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, bilateral cataracts, myopathy, and lactic acidosis, and heart failure was the most severe manifestation.
Sengers syndrome is characterized by congenital cataract, hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, mitochondrial myopathy, and lactic acidosis associated with mutations in AGK gene.

nodes.description: Reduced abundance of sideroflexins and one-carbon enzymes disrupts mitochondrial one-carbon metabolism.

evidence.snippet: Proteomic profiling of Sengers patient fibroblasts and AGK knockout models identifies remodeling of the mitochondrial proteome, including mitochondrial one-carbon metabolism enzymes, inner membrane serine transporters, sideroflexins, and Complex I subunits and assembly factors.

nodes.description: Impaired respiratory chain function with reduced complex I and V activity and decreased oxygen consumption in patient cells.

evidence.snippet: Decreases in the oxygen consumption rate (OCR) and the OCR:ECAR (extracellular acidification rate) ratio in the patient’s fibroblasts indicated reduced electron flow through the respiratory chain, and spectrophotometry revealed decreased activity of OXPHOS complexes I and V.

nodes.description: Disruption of the TIM22 complex reduces import of multi-pass carrier proteins and sideroflexins into the inner mitochondrial membrane.
nodes.id: Reduced TIM22-mediated import of inner-membrane carrier proteins

biological_processes: protein insertion into mitochondrial inner membrane

evidence.snippet: Proteomic profiling of Sengers patient fibroblasts and AGK knockout models identifies remodeling of the mitochondrial proteome, including mitochondrial one-carbon metabolism enzymes, inner membrane serine transporters, sideroflexins, and Complex I subunits and assembly factors.

nodes.description: Disrupted TIM22 complex assembly at the mitochondrial inner membrane impairs the carrier protein import machinery.

evidence.snippet: AGK, a mitochondrial acylglycerol kinase, is not only involved in lipid signaling but is also a component of the TIM22 complex in the inner mitochondrial membrane, which mediates the import of a subset of membrane proteins.
